👥 How to Efficiently Run Your Farm with Fewer Workers

The Post-Brexit Labour Challenge

UK farms have lost access to seasonal EU workers. Wages have risen 15-25%. Many farmers are struggling to find reliable staff. This guide offers practical solutions.

Understanding the Current Situation

Labour Statistics: - 40% of farms report labour shortages - Average wage increases: 20% since 2021 - Seasonal worker scheme: 45,000 visas (insufficient demand) - 25% of farm businesses have reduced output due to labour issues Why it's happening: - Brexit ended free movement - Competing sectors (logistics, hospitality) offer similar pay - Housing costs near farms are unaffordable - Young people leaving rural areas

Automation Solutions

### Robotic Milking Systems Cost: £150,000-£250,000 for 120-cow unit Labour saving: - Before: 3 hours/day milking - After: 30 minutes/day monitoring - Save: 2.5 hours/day = 912 hours/year Payback: 5-7 years (depending on labour costs) Grants available: Farming Investment Fund up to £25,000 ### Autonomous Tractors Options: | System | Cost | Best For | |--------|------|----------| | GPS auto-steer retrofit | £15,000-30,000 | Arable farms | | Full autonomous (John Deere 8R) | £400,000+ | Large operations | | Small robot tractors | £50,000-80,000 | Specialty crops | Labour saving: 30-50% on tractor operations ### Drone Technology See our full guide: [Farm Drones Guide](/equipment/) Quick benefits: - Crop scouting: 6 hours → 30 minutes - Livestock checks: 2 hours → 20 minutes - Mapping: 4 hours → automated Cost: £1,000-£30,000 depending on capability ### Automated Feeding Systems For livestock farms: - Automated feed pushers: £20,000-40,000 - TMR mixers with conveyors: £50,000-100,000 - Individual cow feeders: £5,000-15,000 per unit Labour saving: 1-2 hours/day ### Weeding/Harvesting Robots Emerging technology: - Small Robot Company: £30,000-60,000 per unit - Naïo weeding robots: £20,000-40,000 - Harvesting still limited for most crops

Seasonal Worker Management

### Recruitment Strategies 1. Earlier recruitment - Start recruiting 6 months before season - Contact previous workers by Christmas - Offer return bonuses (£200-500) 2. Expand your search - Partner with agricultural colleges - Contact rural job centres - Use social media (Facebook groups) - Consider non-agricultural workers (training provided) 3. Improve your offer - Guaranteed hours (minimum 40/week) - Overtime paid properly - Accommodation provided or subsidised - End-of-season bonus ### On-Farm Efficiency Job batching: - Group tasks geographically - Minimize travel time - Plan routes efficiently Better tools: - Power tools vs manual - Electric fencing vs post-and-wire - ATV/UTV vs walking Work smarter: - Early starts in summer (avoid heat) - Flexible hours when possible - Job rotation to reduce fatigue

Technology to Reduce Admin

### Farm Management Software Popular options: | Software | Cost | Best For | |----------|------|----------| | Herdwatch | £30/month | Livestock records | | Farmplan | £50/month | Accounts + compliance | | AgriWebb | £40/month | Grazing management | | Fieldmargin | £25/month | Field planning | Time saving: 5-10 hours/week on paperwork ### Automated Compliance - Automatic medicine recording - Digital field records - Online subsidy applications - Integrated accounting

Retention Strategies

### Competitive Pay Current rates (2026): | Role | Hourly Rate | Annual (50hr weeks) | |------|-------------|---------------------| | General farm worker | £12-14 | £31,000-36,000 | | Herdsperson | £14-16 | £36,000-41,000 | | Machinery operator | £13-15 | £33,000-39,000 | | Farm manager | £18-25 | £47,000-65,000 | Additional benefits to offer: - Accommodation (worth £6,000-12,000/year) - Vehicle for work/personal use - Training and qualifications - Meat/milk/eggs allowance - Pension contributions ### Career Development Provide: - Training opportunities (PA1, PA2, ATV, etc.) - Clear progression path - Responsibility and autonomy - Regular feedback and praise ### Work-Life Balance Practical steps: - Rotating weekend duties - Time off during quiet periods - Flexible hours where possible - Respect personal time

Cost-Benefit Analysis Framework

### Calculate Your Labour Cost Current annual labour cost: - Wages: £____ - Accommodation: £____ - NI/pension: £____ - Recruitment: £____ - Training: £____ - Total: £____ ### Automation ROI Calculation Example: Robotic milking - Cost: £200,000 - Grant: £25,000 - Net cost: £175,000 Annual savings: - Labour: £35,000 - Milk quality bonus: £5,000 - Reduced vet costs: £3,000 - Total: £43,000/year Payback: 4.1 years ### Decision Matrix | Solution | Cost | Labour Saved | Payback | Priority | |----------|------|--------------|---------|----------| | Robotic milking | High | Very High | 4-7 years | High (if >80 cows) | | Auto-steer | Medium | Medium | 3-5 years | High | | Drones | Low | Low | 1-2 years | Medium | | Feed automation | Medium | Medium | 5-8 years | Medium | | Software | Low | Medium | Immediate | Very High |

Immediate Actions (This Week)

1. Audit current labour usage: - Track time spent on each task - Identify biggest time sinks - Spot inefficiencies 2. Review recruitment: - Update job adverts - Contact previous workers - Check accommodation standards 3. Research grants: - Farming Investment Fund eligibility - SFI productivity actions - Local council schemes 4. Technology demo: - Book robotic milking demo - Trial farm management software - Attend drone workshop

Funding Available

Farming Investment Fund: - Equipment automation - Technology upgrades - Up to £25,000 grants SFI Actions: - Precision farming techniques - Reduced input approaches - Payment for innovation Rural Development Programme: - Training grants - Business advice - Local schemes vary

Get Help

- AHDB Labour Guide: ahdb.org.uk/labour - NFU Employment Advice: nfuonline.com - ACAS Helpline: 0300 123 1100 - Agricultural solicitor: For contracts/compliance *Labour is your biggest cost and biggest challenge. Automation isn't about replacing people – it's about making your farm sustainable with the workforce available.* *Last updated: 26 February 2026*
